## 📋 Pull Request Checklist

### 🧪 Testing
- [ ] I have added tests that cover my changes
- [ ] All new and existing tests pass
- [ ] I have run the test suite locally: `pytest tests/ -v`
- [ ] I have run integration tests (if applicable)

### 📝 Code Quality
- [ ] Code follows the project's style guidelines
- [ ] I have run `ruff check .` and fixed all issues
- [ ] I have run `ruff format .` to format the code
- [ ] I have run `mypy skillforge/` for type checking
- [ ] I have added type hints where appropriate

### 📚 Documentation
- [ ] I have updated the relevant documentation
- [ ] I have added docstrings for new functions/classes
- [ ] I have updated the CHANGELOG.md if this is a user-facing change
- [ ] README.md is updated if needed

### 🔒 Security
- [ ] I have considered security implications of my changes
- [ ] I have run `skillforge scan` on any test skills
- [ ] No hardcoded credentials or secrets are included
- [ ] Dependencies are secure and up-to-date

### 🚀 Ready to Merge
- [ ] My code follows the project's contribution guidelines
- [ ] I have self-reviewed my own code
- [ ] I have made corresponding changes to the documentation
- [ ] My changes generate no new warnings
- [ ] My changes do not break existing functionality

---

## 📝 Description

### 🎯 What does this PR do?
Briefly describe the changes made in this pull request.

### 🔄 Changes Made
- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] New feature
- [ ] Breaking change
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Performance improvement
- [ ] Security improvement
- [ ] Other: _______

### 📋 Type of Change
- [ ] 🐛 Bug fix (non-breaking change that fixes an issue)
- [ ] ✨ New feature (non-breaking change that adds functionality)
- [ ] 💥 Breaking change (fix or feature that would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)
- [ ] 📚 Documentation update
- [ ] 🎨 Code style/quality improvement
- [ ] 🔒 Security improvement
- [ ] ⚡ Performance improvement

### 🧪 Testing Strategy
How did you test your changes?
- Unit tests
- Integration tests
- Manual testing
- Performance testing
- Security testing

### 📊 Screenshots (if applicable)
Add screenshots to help explain your changes.

### 🔗 Related Issues
Fixes #123
Closes #456
Related to #789

### 📚 Additional Context
Add any other context about the pull request here.

---

## 📖 Reviewer Guidance

### 🎯 Areas to Focus On
- [ ] Logic correctness
- [ ] Security implications
- [ ] Performance impact
- [ ] Code readability
- [ ] Documentation completeness

### 🧪 Test These Scenarios
1. Test scenario 1
2. Test scenario 2
3. Test scenario 3

### 📋 Checklist for Reviewers
- [ ] Code is well-written and easy to understand
- [ ] Tests are comprehensive and appropriate
- [ ] Documentation is clear and accurate
- [ ] Changes are backward compatible (if not breaking)
- [ ] Security implications have been considered
- [ ] Performance impact is acceptable

---

**Thank you for contributing to SkillForge! 🎉**
